key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings denser and more wear-resistant, though with a slightly higher friction coefficient.
engineering_relevance: This suggests barrel filling ratio is a practical process knob for tuning protective carbide coatings used in high-wear and high-temperature components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under identical conditions
Compared microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and XRD
Evaluated dry sliding wear with ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N, 5 and 10 cm/s
evidence_signal: The 53% coating showed lower porosity, retained Cr3C2/NiCr as dominant phases with minor Cr7C3 formation, and achieved a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N).
watch_item: The improvement comes with higher friction, and the results are based on a limited set of loads and sliding speeds, so broader operating validation is still needed.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
